name: Visual Strategy Artefacts & Deck Patterns Ontology
alternateName: VIZSTRAT-ONT — Templates, Decks, Diagrams, Data Source Mapping & Skeleton Diagrams
description: The capstone SC ontology. Formalizes the visual output layer of strategy communication: 9 single-artefact templates (One Slider, Use Case Map, Directional Costing, Priority Map, Technology Radar, Build/Buy/Partner, Due Diligence, Audit & Traceability, Architectural Definition), 6 composed deck patterns (Ghost, Ask, Strategy, Roadmap, Tactical Plan, Strategy Plan), Mermaid diagram patterns, data source mappings to SA ontology entities, audience-appropriate view configurations, skeleton diagrams for idea-to-execution graph traversal, and artefact versioning for evolution tracking. Templates are atomic; decks are composed. An AI agent selects deck pattern based on communication objective, applies SC communication pattern, renders templates from SA data, and assembles into audience-appropriate delivery. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication).
oaa:domain: Strategy Visualisation, Communication Artefacts, Decision Support

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Objective-to-Artefact Pipeline
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Full pipeline from communication objective to rendered artefact: select deck pattern, select communication pattern, render templates from SA data, assemble into audience-appropriate delivery.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: nar:CommunicationObjective → viz:DeckPattern [WHERE objective + audience match] → viz:deckComposedOf → viz:DeckComposition → viz:compositionHasTemplate → viz:ArtefactTemplate → viz:templateHasDataSource → viz:DataSourceMapping → [SA entities]
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: End-to-end artefact generation from communication objective

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Skeleton-to-Execution Traversal
oaa:joinPatterns.description: From strategic question through MECE decomposition, SA analysis, SC communication, to execution artefacts. The skeleton diagram provides the structural outline; the pipeline fills it with data.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: rsn:StrategicQuestion → viz:skeletonStartsFromQuestion → viz:SkeletonDiagram → [rsn:MECETree → SA analysis → pfl:StrategicDirectionSummary → nar:StrategicNarrative → viz:DeckPattern → okr:Objective → ppm:Initiative]
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Trace from initial strategic idea to execution plan via visual skeleton

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Artefact Evolution Tracking
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Track how visual artefacts change between strategic review cycles. Each review cycle produces updated artefacts; versions enable diff comparison.
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Strategy evolution visualisation — what changed and why

entities.name: VisualArtefact
entities.description: Base entity for any rendered visual strategy output. A VisualArtefact is a concrete, audience-targeted communication deliverable generated from SA data through SC patterns. It has a template type, data sources, an audience view, and a version history. Artefacts can be standalone (single template) or composed into decks.

oaa:properties.name: templateRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:ArtefactTemplate @id defining the type

oaa:properties.name: narrativeRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:StrategicNarrative @id providing narrative context

oaa:properties.name: audienceViewRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:AudienceView @id for audience-appropriate rendering

oaa:properties.name: format
oaa:properties.description: Output format

entities.name: ArtefactTemplate
entities.description: One of 9 single-artefact template types. Each template defines a focused view of a specific strategic dimension. Templates are atomic — they produce one artefact from specific SA data sources for a specific audience. Templates can be composed into deck patterns for structured communication sequences.

oaa:properties.name: templateCode
oaa:properties.description: Machine-readable template type

oaa:properties.name: purpose
oaa:properties.description: What this template communicates and why

oaa:properties.name: primaryAudience
oaa:properties.description: Primary audience levels for this template

oaa:properties.name: diagramPatternRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:DiagramPattern @id for Mermaid rendering

OneSlider.purpose: Entire strategic direction on a single page — where we play, how we win, what we measure, who owns what

UseCaseMap.purpose: Visual map of strategic use cases — who does what, in what context, to achieve what outcome

DirectionalCosting.purpose: Rough-order cost model for strategic initiatives — direction, not precision

PriorityMap.purpose: 2x2 or ranked view of strategic priorities by impact vs effort

TechnologyRadar.purpose: ThoughtWorks-style radar: Adopt / Trial / Assess / Hold

BuildBuyPartner.purpose: Decision framework for capability sourcing: build, buy, or partner

DueDiligence.purpose: Structured assessment of a strategic opportunity, acquisition, or partnership

AuditTraceability.purpose: End-to-end traceability from strategic direction through to execution

ArchitecturalDefinition.purpose: Strategic architecture view: capability map, technology landscape, target state

entities.name: DeckPattern
entities.description: One of 6 composed deck patterns. Decks are ordered sequences of templates assembled for a specific communication purpose with a specific SC communication pattern. Each deck has a structural logic — not just a slide order. An AI agent selects a deck pattern based on communication objective and audience, then renders each template from SA data.

oaa:properties.name: deckCode
oaa:properties.description: Machine-readable deck type

oaa:properties.name: purpose
oaa:properties.description: Communication purpose of this deck pattern

oaa:properties.name: structuralLogic
oaa:properties.description: How the deck is structured (e.g. 'Context → Problem → Options → Recommendation → Ask → Next Steps')

oaa:properties.name: communicationPatternRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:CommunicationPattern @id governing overall approach

oaa:properties.name: primaryAudience
oaa:properties.description: Primary audience levels

oaa:properties.name: templateCount
oaa:properties.description: Typical number of templates in this deck

GhostDeck.purpose: Pre-meeting alignment — circulated before so attendees arrive prepared
GhostDeck.structuralLogic: Direction summary → Key questions → Decision points → Pre-read data

AskDeck.purpose: Secure a specific decision, approval, or resource
AskDeck.structuralLogic: Context (30 sec) → Problem/opportunity → Options evaluated → Recommendation → The Ask → Next steps

StrategyDeck.purpose: Comprehensive strategic direction presentation — the full narrative
StrategyDeck.structuralLogic: Narrative arc: Context → Challenge → Choice → Commitment → Cadence

Roadmap.structuralLogic: Now → Next → Later (or Q1-Q4 / H1-H2 / Year 1-3)

TacticalPlan.purpose: Near-term execution detail per team or initiative
TacticalPlan.structuralLogic: Objective → Actions → Owners → Timeline → Success criteria → Dependencies → Risks

StrategyPlan.purpose: Full strategic planning document — the authoritative reference
StrategyPlan.structuralLogic: Executive summary → Full narrative arc → SA summaries → BSC + OKR cascade → Investment map → Roadmap → Governance → Appendices

entities.name: DeckComposition
entities.description: Ordered sequence of artefact templates within a deck. Each entry in the composition specifies which template is rendered, its position in the sequence, and narrative transition guidance (how it connects to the next template).

oaa:properties.name: deckPatternRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:DeckPattern @id

oaa:properties.name: templateRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:ArtefactTemplate @id

oaa:properties.name: sequenceOrder
oaa:properties.description: Position in the deck (1-based)

oaa:properties.name: narrativeTransition
oaa:properties.description: How to transition from this template to the next (e.g. 'Having seen what it costs, let us look at when it happens')

oaa:properties.name: optional
oaa:properties.description: Whether this template can be omitted based on audience or context

entities.name: DiagramPattern
entities.description: Mermaid/graph diagram rendering pattern for a specific artefact template. Defines the preferred diagram type, layout direction, node and edge semantics, and structural rules. Diagrams are first-class SC artefacts — they make strategy structure visible, traversable, and shareable.

oaa:properties.name: templateRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:ArtefactTemplate @id this pattern renders

oaa:properties.name: mermaidType
oaa:properties.description: Preferred Mermaid diagram type

oaa:properties.name: layoutDirection
oaa:properties.description: Preferred layout direction for flowcharts

oaa:properties.name: nodeSemantics
oaa:properties.description: What nodes represent in this diagram pattern

oaa:properties.name: edgeSemantics
oaa:properties.description: What edges represent in this diagram pattern

oaa:properties.name: structuralRules
oaa:properties.description: Constraints on the diagram structure (e.g. 'DAG only', 'max 4 levels deep')

DirectionalCosting.nodeSemantics: Total budget → Themes → Initiatives → Cost ranges

PriorityMap.nodeSemantics: Strategic priorities plotted by impact vs effort

TechnologyRadar.nodeSemantics: Concentric rings: Adopt / Trial / Assess / Hold

entities.name: DataSourceMapping
entities.description: Mapping from SA ontology entities to visual artefact data inputs. Each template has specific SA data requirements — DataSourceMapping makes this explicit and machine-traversable. An AI agent follows these mappings to pull the right data from the right SA ontologies when rendering a template.

oaa:properties.name: templateRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:ArtefactTemplate @id

oaa:properties.name: saEntityRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to SA ontology entity @id (e.g. pfl:StrategicDirectionSummary)

oaa:properties.name: saOntologyName
oaa:properties.description: Name of the SA ontology providing data (e.g. PORTFOLIO-ONT)

oaa:properties.name: dataRole
oaa:properties.description: Role of this data in the artefact

oaa:properties.name: requiredProperties
oaa:properties.description: Specific properties from the SA entity needed for rendering

oaa:properties.name: transformationNotes
oaa:properties.description: How the SA data should be transformed for this artefact

entities.name: AudienceView
entities.description: Audience-appropriate detail level configuration for an artefact. The same data can be rendered at different levels of detail for different audiences. AudienceView controls what is shown, what is hidden, and how data is aggregated for each cascade level. Implements progressive disclosure: start with direction summary, drill into BSC perspectives, zoom to team OKRs.

oaa:properties.name: templateRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:ArtefactTemplate @id

oaa:properties.name: cascadeLevelRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to csc:CascadeLevel @id this view is configured for

oaa:properties.name: visibleElements
oaa:properties.description: Which artefact elements are visible at this detail level

oaa:properties.name: hiddenElements
oaa:properties.description: Which artefact elements are hidden at this detail level

oaa:properties.name: aggregationRules
oaa:properties.description: How data is rolled up for this audience (e.g. 'show portfolio totals not individual initiative costs')

entities.name: ArtefactVersion
entities.description: A versioned snapshot of a visual artefact tied to a strategic review cycle. Each review cycle may produce updated artefacts. Versions enable diff comparison: what changed in the visual representation between cycles, reflecting how the underlying strategy evolved.

oaa:properties.name: artefactRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to viz:VisualArtefact @id

oaa:properties.name: reviewCycleRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:StrategicReviewCycle @id that triggered this version

oaa:properties.name: changesSummary
oaa:properties.description: Summary of what changed from previous version

entities.name: SkeletonDiagram
entities.description: A structural outline for idea-to-execution graph traversal. A skeleton diagram is the 'napkin sketch' that becomes a traversable graph — it scopes the structural outline of a strategic idea before full SA analysis or SC communication. The progression: Idea → Skeleton → SA analysis → SC communication → Execution. Each node in the skeleton references an ontology entity type; each edge references a relationship type. AI agents generate skeletons from strategic questions; humans refine before full analysis.

oaa:properties.name: strategicQuestionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rsn:StrategicQuestion @id this skeleton explores

oaa:properties.name: mermaidCode
oaa:properties.description: Mermaid diagram code for the skeleton

oaa:properties.name: nodeEntityTypes
oaa:properties.description: Ontology entity types referenced in skeleton nodes

oaa:properties.name: scopeDescription
oaa:properties.description: What strategic territory this skeleton covers

relationships.name: Artefact Uses Template
relationships.description: A visual artefact is an instance of an artefact template

relationships.name: Template Has Data Source
relationships.description: Artefact template has data source mappings to SA ontology entities

relationships.name: Template Has Diagram Pattern
relationships.description: Artefact template has a Mermaid diagram rendering pattern

relationships.name: Deck Composed Of
relationships.description: Deck pattern is composed of ordered template entries

relationships.name: Composition Has Template
relationships.description: Deck composition entry references a specific artefact template

relationships.name: Deck Uses Pattern
relationships.description: Deck pattern uses a narrative communication pattern for overall structural approach

relationships.name: Deck Targets Audience
relationships.description: Deck pattern targets specific cascade audience levels

relationships.name: Artefact Has Audience View
relationships.description: Visual artefact has audience-specific view configurations

relationships.name: Skeleton Starts From Question
relationships.description: Skeleton diagram starts from a strategic question for idea-to-execution traversal

relationships.name: Artefact Renders From SA
relationships.description: Data source mapping references SA ontology entities as data input

businessRules.name: Artefact Must Have Template
businessRules.action: THEN templateRef MUST reference a valid viz:ArtefactTemplate @id

businessRules.name: Deck Must Have Composition
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least two DeckComposition entries (a deck with one template is just a template)

businessRules.name: Data Source Must Reference Valid SA Entity
businessRules.action: THEN saEntityRef MUST reference a valid entity @id from the specified saOntologyName

businessRules.name: Skeleton Must Reference Strategic Question
businessRules.action: THEN strategicQuestionRef MUST reference a valid rsn:StrategicQuestion @id

businessRules.name: Published Artefact Must Have Approved Version
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ArtefactVersion with status 'Approved' or 'Published'

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of VIZSTRAT-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication). Capstone ontology with 15 imports — the widest dependency graph in the library. Formalizes 9 artefact templates, 6 deck patterns, diagram patterns, data source mappings, audience views, skeleton diagrams, and artefact versioning. Cross-references all SC ontologies plus VSOM-SA pipeline.
